<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
**Debugging session issues**

I'm looking into why `authClient.useSession` is returning user as undefined. This likely happens because the session is either pending or not set. I noticed that `console.log` fires before the pending check, so I should consider handling cases where `isPending` is true or where the session is null. The initial render shows the session as undefined until it's fully loaded. I might also need to ensure that the auth provider is set correctly in the root layout for this library. I'll check the `lib/auth-client` next.</details></think>

_**Agent (default)**_

Session being `undefined` is usually expected on the **first render** (the hook is still loading), *or* it can stay undefined if the app is missing the required provider/cookies. Next I’ll check your `authClient` setup and whether the provider is mounted in your root layout.

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
**Addressing auth client issues**

I’m identifying some problems with the auth client. It exports authClient with the production base URL, but also signIn from createAuthClient() without a base URL, which seems like a bug. Plus, authClient.useSession always points to the production URL, impacting cookies on localhost during development. I think it's best to set the base URL as an environment variable and create it once. I should also check if the provider for better-auth/react useSession is really necessary. I’ll review the layouts for providers.</details></think>

**Implementing minor changes**

It seems I’m currently pending with this task. I should explain why I'll make minor changes in the Navbar and profile—specifically to log when the session is resolved. _**Agent (default)**_

I found a concrete reason your session can stay `undefined`: your `authClient` is hardcoded to call **`https://libris-next.vercel.app`** (production) for session data, so when you run locally your browser cookies/session on `localhost` won’t be sent there. I’m going to fix `src/lib/auth-client.js` to use same-origin by default (or an env var), and remove the accidental second `createAuthClient()` instance.

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
**Adjusting UI elements**

I'm thinking about the layout issues I'm facing. The "w-full" class seems to take the full width of its parent, which might cause overflow if the parent isn't defined with a width. It looks like I need to change the CheckboxGroup to use a flex column with spacing. I’ll also set the Surface's width to around 320 pixels and add some overflow control. Finally, I should patch the FilterBooks component to apply these changes. I'll proceed with modifying both files as necessary!</details></think>

_**Agent (default)**_

I found the cause: `FilterBooks` uses `w-full` inside a `shrink-0` wrapper with no fixed width, so the card can collapse and the HeroUI checkbox layout gets squeezed. I’ll give the filter card a stable width + overflow handling, and set the checkbox group to a clean vertical layout with consistent gaps (matching your screenshot).

Adding horizontal layout to each `Checkbox` root: HeroUI likely defaults the control and label to block layout; `flex flex-row items-center gap-3` aligns them like a standard checkbox row.

_**Agent (default)**_

Here’s what was going wrong: `CheckboxGroup` was only controlling **vertical spacing between rows**. Each `Checkbox` still laid out `Checkbox.Control` and `Checkbox.Content` as **block-level** siblings, so the box and label stacked on separate lines.

**Change:** put each row in a horizontal flex on the `Checkbox` root so the indicator and label sit on one line, while the group stays a column of rows:

```51:58:C:\Users\KIIT\Desktop\Programming Hero\Milestone 8\libris-next\src\components\FilterBooks.jsx
                <Checkbox value="all" className="flex flex-row items-center gap-3">
                    <Checkbox.Control>
                        <Checkbox.Indicator />
                    </Checkbox.Control>
                    <Checkbox.Content>
                        <Label>All Books</Label>
                    </Checkbox.Content>
                </Checkbox>
```

The same `className="flex flex-row items-center gap-3"` is on the Story, Tech, and Science checkboxes too.

If anything still stacks, say so—then the library may be putting `className` on an inner node, and we can wrap each option in a `div` with that flex layout instead.
